notes: Two independent incidents fire in the same window. REAL/paged: payments-db connection pool saturates (active connections approach max_connections after commit c41d9f02 added a synchronous fraud-check query that holds a connection per request); payments-svc then fails acquiring connections (FATAL: remaining connection slots / acquire timeout, returns 503) and checkout at the edge returns customer-facing 5xx (the page). UNRELATED: analytics-worker crashloops after deploy v412 (commit 9b2e..) and is the LOUDEST by error count, and analytics-api (its only caller) logs errors too. But analytics-worker/analytics-api are an internal batch path with NO edge to payments-svc/checkout and no customer impact. The trap: analytics-worker errored most AND just deployed, so it is the tempting origin; it is a separate self-contained incident, not the cause of the checkout cascade. Controls: search-svc and notifications-svc stay healthy.
_design_note: Paged on checkout (customer 5xx) which is the edge victim of the payments cascade. analytics-worker is the loudest+just-deployed surface match but belongs to a parallel incident; it must NOT be conflated into the payments chain or named as origin.
